description.task: Write a Java program that converts a temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit, with proper single-line comments and a Javadoc comment explaining the code.
instructions: Use 37.0 as the Celsius value (normal human body temperature).
Write a Javadoc comment (/** ... */) at the top of the class explaining what the program does.
Add at least one single-line comment (//) inside main() explaining the formula.
Print the result in the exact format: '37.0C = 98.6F'
description.hint: Use 9.0 / 5.0 (with .0) instead of 9 / 5 to avoid integer division. Comments have no effect on program output — only System.out.println() produces output.

description.task: The starter code is a working program but has zero comments. Add meaningful comments to explain every section of the code.
description.context: Reading and annotating existing code is a real developer skill — you will often need to understand code written by others.
instructions: Do NOT change any of the logic or variable names in the code.
Add a single-line comment above the class explaining what the program does overall.
Add a comment before 'int n = 10;' explaining what n represents.
Add a comment before the for-loop explaining what it does.
Add a comment on the line 'sum = sum + i;' explaining the accumulation.
The output must remain the same: 55.
description.hint: This program calculates the sum of all integers from 1 to 10. The answer is 1+2+3+...+10 = 55.

title: Javadoc for a Simple Calculator Class

description.task: Write a Java class called SimpleCalculator that has two static methods (add and multiply). Add proper Javadoc comments to the class and both methods.
description.context: Javadoc is a documentation standard used in professional Java development. IDEs like IntelliJ use Javadoc to show tooltips when you hover over methods.
instructions: Replace the '// Add your class-level Javadoc here' placeholder with a proper /** ... */ Javadoc block.
Add a Javadoc comment before each method that includes a @param tag for each parameter and a @return tag.
Do NOT change the method logic.
The program must still print the expected output.
description.hint:
Javadoc format: /** 
 * Description.
 * @param a first number
 * @return the result
 */
